ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit theft via ntdsutil IFM snapshot on a Domain Controller
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Volt Typhoon (a.k.a. Vanguard Panda / VOLTZITE) hands-on-keyboard credential-access step where the operator drops to a DC console and runs `ntdsutil` with the `ifm` (Install From Media) sub-command to create a full Active Directory snapshot — yielding NTDS.dit and the SYSTEM hive without touching LSASS. CISA AA24-038A, Microsoft (Volt Typhoon advisory) and CrowdStrike's Vanguard Panda intrusion notes both call this out as the actor's preferred DCSync-free domain hash dump.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=ntdsutil.exe OR Processes.original_file_name=ntdsutil.exe) Processes.user!=*$ Processes.process IN ("*ifm*","*create full*","*create*ntds*","*ac i ntds*","*activate instance ntds*") by host Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user _time span=1m | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Filesystem.file_path) as drop_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as drop_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("ntds.dit","SYSTEM","SECURITY") Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\Public\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*") by host _time span=1m | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")`] | where isnotnull(drop_files) | table firstTime host process_name parent user process drop_path drop_files
ucs.kql:
// Volt Typhoon — ntdsutil IFM snapshot used to dump NTDS.dit on a DC.
// The actor consistently runs the IFM flow off cmd.exe on the DC console.
let _lookback = 14d;
let _ntds_proc =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    // catch renamed binaries — Vanguard Panda has been observed copying ntdsutil out of System32
    | where FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
        or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
        or ProcessVersionInfoInternalFileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ifm", "create full", "create ntds", "ac i ntds", "activate instance ntds")
    | project NtdsTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
              NtdsCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, IsRemote = InitiatingProcessAccountName != AccountName;
// Cross-confirm with the artefact dropping on disk in a non-standard staging directory
let _ntds_drop =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName in~ ("ntds.dit", "SYSTEM", "SECURITY")
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
        or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile = FileName, DropPath = FolderPath,
              DropProc = InitiatingProcessFileName;
_ntds_proc
| join kind=leftouter _ntds_drop on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or (DropTime between (NtdsTime - 5m .. NtdsTime + 30m))
| project NtdsTime, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain, NtdsCmd, ParentImage, ParentCmd,
          DropTime, DroppedFile, DropPath, DropProc
| order by NtdsTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volt Typhoon — ntdsutil IFM snapshot on a DC (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688 + 4663).
// Requires GPO 'Include command line in process creation events'.
let _lookback = 14d;
let _ntds_proc =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\ntdsutil.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("ifm", "create full", "create ntds", "ac i ntds", "activate instance ntds")
    | project NtdsTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NtdsCmd = CommandLine,
              ParentImage = ParentProcessName, NewProcessId, SubjectLogonId;
let _ntds_drop =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
    | where EventID == 4663                                     // file write audit
    | where ObjectName has_any ("ntds.dit", @"\SYSTEM", @"\SECURITY")
    | where ObjectName has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, DroppedFile = ObjectName, DropProc = ProcessName;
_ntds_proc
| join kind=leftouter _ntds_drop on Computer
| where isnull(DropTime) or (DropTime between (NtdsTime - 5m .. NtdsTime + 30m))
| project NtdsTime, Computer, Account, NtdsCmd, ParentImage, DropTime, DroppedFile, DropProc
| order by NtdsTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit Dump via ntdsutil IFM Snapshot
id: 6e2d5b21-7e4a-4c2b-9b0e-volttyphoon-ifm
status: experimental
description: Detects ntdsutil being invoked with the IFM (Install From Media) sub-command sequence used by Volt Typhoon / Vanguard Panda to extract NTDS.dit + SYSTEM hive from a Domain Controller without touching LSASS.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://www.crowdstrike.com/blog/falcon-complete-zero-day-exploit-chain/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.003
  - threat-actor.volt_typhoon
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\ntdsutil.exe'
  selection_orig:
    OriginalFileName: 'ntdsutil.exe'
  selection_cli:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' ifm'
      - 'create full'
      - 'create ntds'
      - 'ac i ntds'
      - 'activate instance ntds'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_img or selection_orig) and selection_cli and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate AD admin restoring/seeding a new DC via IFM (rare; should be ticketed)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic NTDS-dump detections fire on Mimikatz, secretsdump, DCSync, etc. — Volt Typhoon specifically and consistently chooses the native ntdsutil IFM path because (a) it is signed Microsoft, (b) it does not interact with LSASS so it bypasses MDE/CrowdStrike credential-dump heuristics, and (c) it's their LOTL doctrine. Pairing the `ifm`/`create full` sub-command shape with an artefact drop into Pub
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon LOTL discovery burst — chained native recon under one shell
ucs.description: Hunts Volt Typhoon's characteristic 'living-off-the-land' reconnaissance burst on a freshly-accessed host: a single non-machine account, almost always running under cmd.exe or powershell.exe spawned from a web-shell or proxy process, executes 4+ of the actor's documented native discovery binaries (net, netstat, tasklist, systeminfo, whoami, ipconfig, query, nltest, wmic, ping) within a few minutes. CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft's Volt Typhoon write-up reproduce the exact command list — this is their signature pre-credential-access fingerprint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as bins values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_recon_bins from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.user!=*$ Processes.parent_process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","sqlservr.exe","ManageEngine*.exe","fortiweb*.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","netstat.exe","tasklist.exe","systeminfo.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","query.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","wmic.exe","ping.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","reg.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=5m | where distinct_recon_bins >= 4 | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | rename host as dest | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name distinct_recon_bins bins cmds | sort - distinct_recon_bins firstTime
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volt Typhoon — LOTL recon burst on Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688.
// Requires command-line auditing GPO.
let _lookback = 7d;
let _bucket = 5m;
let _recon_bins = dynamic([
    @"\net.exe", @"\net1.exe", @"\netstat.exe", @"\tasklist.exe", @"\systeminfo.exe",
    @"\whoami.exe", @"\ipconfig.exe", @"\query.exe", @"\quser.exe", @"\nltest.exe",
    @"\wmic.exe", @"\ping.exe", @"\arp.exe", @"\route.exe", @"\reg.exe", @"\hostname.exe"
]);
let _shell_parents = dynamic([
    @"\cmd.exe", @"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe",
    @"\w3wp.exe", @"\httpd.exe", @"\tomcat9.exe", @"\java.exe", @"\sqlservr.exe",
    @"\manageengineservicedesk.exe", @"\fortiwebd.exe"
]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(_lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend NewProcLower    = tolower(NewProcessName),
         ParentProcLower = tolower(ParentProcessName)
| where NewProcLower    has_any (_recon_bins)
| where ParentProcLower has_any (_shell_parents)
| extend ReconBin = extract(@"([^\\]+\.exe)$", 1, NewProcLower)
| summarize ReconBinsCount = dcount(ReconBin),
            ReconBins      = make_set(ReconBin, 32),
            CmdLines       = make_set(CommandLine, 64),
            ParentChain    = make_set(ParentProcessName, 8),
            FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated)
            by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, _bucket)
| where ReconBinsCount >= 4
| extend BurstWindowSec = datetime_diff('second', LastSeen, FirstSeen)
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, BurstWindowSec, Computer, Account,
          ReconBinsCount, ReconBins, ParentChain, CmdLines
| order by ReconBinsCount desc, FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Volt Typhoon is the most prominent LOTL-only actor and CISA AA24-038A explicitly enumerates this exact discovery-binary set as their fingerprint — they avoid implants and rely on built-in tooling on internet-edge appliances and DCs. A help-desk admin or sysadmin will run *one* of these tools, not 4+ in a 5-minute burst under a single shell parent. Anchoring the parent process to web-shell-hosting
